findings.summary: Claude Code supports headless sessions via '-p' flag. Latest patterns (2026) favor persistent stream-json mode for subprocess communication to avoid reload costs. Key gotchas: nested CLAUDECODE env vars conflict with subprocesses (mitigated by unset), full config propagation wastes tokens in nested scenarios.
sd_sections_updated: 2.5 Future Optimization: Agent SDK Hooks Approach

findings.summary: Agent SDK spawns Claude Code CLI via anyio.open_process() with JSON-RPC bidirectional communication. Hooks (PreToolUse, PostToolUse, SessionStart/End) provide lifecycle management and audit trails. Research validates SDK hooks would be superior to file-based monitoring for long-running workers (10-60min sessions) due to native orphan prevention and low-latency callbacks. Current SD uses Popen+filesystem approach (valid but suboptimal).
sd_sections_updated: 2.5 Future Optimization: Agent SDK Hooks Approach

sd_changes_summary: Added section 2.5 documenting Agent SDK hooks as superior architectural alternative for future refactoring; added section 2.6 with 5 implementation gotchas (nested CLAUDECODE, config propagation, stdout suppression, signal handling, env isolation) discovered via current 2026 research.

architectural_insights.current_approach: Direct Popen + filesystem-based monitoring + file signals (sdk_monitor.py)
architectural_insights.why_current: Minimal dependencies, proven pattern, works for short-lived sessions
architectural_insights.future_optimization: Agent SDK hooks provide native lifecycle management, orphan prevention, structured audit trails, low-latency bidirectional callbacks
architectural_insights.when_to_migrate: When worker sessions exceed 30+ minutes or observability becomes critical; acceptable to defer until Epic 5+
architectural_insights.migration_cost: Medium - requires replacing Popen spawn with SDK client, converting status checks to hook callbacks, removing file signal protocol

gotchas: NESTED_CLAUDECODE: Worker subprocess must have CLAUDECODE env var unset (via env={"CLAUDECODE": ""})
CONFIG_PROPAGATION: Use '--settings-sources project' flag when spawning worker to avoid inheriting ~/CLAUDE.md and wasting 5K+ tokens
STDOUT_SUPPRESSION: If runner launches worker via Bash tool instead of direct Popen, stdout is suppressed and progress detection breaks - use direct subprocess spawn
SIGNAL_HANDLING: Long-running sessions (30+ min) need adaptive monitoring intervals to avoid false STUCK detection
ENV_ISOLATION: Use explicit env dict in Popen to avoid inheriting parent's LD_PRELOAD, PYTHONPATH, other leaky globals
validation_status: CURRENT - All patterns validated via Context7 docs (claude-code v2.1.39, claude-agent-sdk-python v1.0+) and Perplexity research (March 2026 best practices). No breaking changes detected between 2025-2026.

references.perplexity_headless_patterns: March 2026 best practices for headless Claude Code spawning
references.perplexity_architectural_analysis: Subprocess monitoring tradeoffs analysis (Approach A vs B)

notes_for_implementer: The current SD design (Popen+filesystem) is valid and sufficient for Epic 2. However, implementers should be aware of the 5 gotchas documented in section 2.6, especially NESTED_CLAUDECODE and CONFIG_PROPAGATION which will cause immediate failures if overlooked. Consider using explicit '--settings-sources project' flag to reduce token waste in worker spawns. The Agent SDK hooks alternative remains available for future work if long-running worker sessions become a priority.
